findings: Backward and forward bulk-related features form localized clusters with near-constant values and extremely low cardinality across partitions.
Pairwise dependency relations among these features are undefined, preventing dependency concentration or stability assessment.
Partition-level data quality issues such as duplicated rows and class imbalance may influence structural patterns and dependency signals.
limitations: Dependency analysis limited by near-constant feature values and undefined correlations.
No external dataset comparison possible due to lack of finite dependency signals.
